LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT MR. ROSENQUEST IS A JUDGE PRO TEM FOR THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT; SHE DOES NOT KNOW MR. ROSENQUEST BY SIGHT BUT KNOWS HIM BY NAME. SHE MAY HAVE SPOKEN TO HIM ONCE OR TWICE, BUT SHE DOES NOT HAVE ANY RELATIONSHIP WITH HIM OUTSIDE THE COURT. THIS FACT WILL NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. DEFENDANT JJJ, LLC'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS IS GRANTED. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Set For Trial On Jul-06-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nils Rosenquest, Esq. of Rosenquest & Associates, T: 415.292.0980, for defendant and moving party;
Rachel K. Brilliant, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming LLP, T:415.524.4300, for defendant and moving party;
Joe Elford, Esq. of the Law Offices of Joe Elford, T:415.573.7842, for plaintiff Tariq Alazraie, also present in court.

Before hearing, Judge Miller disclosed that Mr. Rosenquest is a Judge Pro Tem for the San Francisco Superior Court; she does not know Mr. Rosenquest by sight but knows him by name. She may have spoken to him once or twice, but she does not have any relationship with him outside the court. This fact will not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. No questions were asked or objections were raised.

The matter was argued. The Court granted plaintiff's counsel's request to allow oral testimony of Mr. Alazraie. Mr. Alazraie was sworn, examined and cross examined. After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT JJJ, LLC'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS is granted. Request for attorney's fees and costs is denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Maria L. Horcasitas, CSR# 8683, T:415.227.4030.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearance. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is transferred to Department 302 to be heard on the same date and time per the order of the presiding judge. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Smith, Not Reported (501)
